description: Sistema para edição e replicação de fotos de anúncios ML em massa. Usuário informa MLB, edita fotos (excluir/adicionar/reordenar), busca por SKU e aplica o conjunto editado em todos os anúncios com mesmo SKU.

userStories.title: Database migration — photo_logs table
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a photo_logs table so that photo operations are tracked per org.
acceptanceCriteria: Create migration file app/db/migrations/016_photo_logs.sql

Table photo_logs has columns: id (bigserial PK), user_id (uuid FK→users), org_id (uuid FK→orgs), source_item_id (text), sku (text), targets (jsonb), total_targets (int default 0), success_count (int default 0), error_count (int default 0), status (text default 'pending'), created_at (timestamptz default now())
targets JSONB stores array of objects: {seller_slug, item_id, status, error}
Add index on org_id and created_at
FK org_id ON DELETE SET NULL (nullable), FK user_id ON DELETE SET NULL (nullable) — same pattern as compat_logs
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow the same pattern as compat_logs (migration 001). Next migration number is 016. Apply via Supabase MCP. Project ID: wrbrbhuhsaaupqsimkqz.

userStories.title: Backend — photo preview endpoint
userStories.description: As a user, I want an endpoint to fetch photos and SKUs from an MLB item so I can see them before editing.
acceptanceCriteria: Create app/routers/photos.py with APIRouter(prefix='/api/photos', tags=['photos'])
GET /api/photos/preview/{item_id} endpoint with optional query param 'seller'
Uses require_active_org dependency for auth
If no seller provided, tries all connected sellers in parallel (reuse _resolve_item_seller pattern from compat.py)
Returns JSON: {id, title, thumbnail, pictures: [{id, url, secure_url, size}], skus: [string], seller: string}
Pictures extracted from item.pictures array, each with id/url/secure_url/size fields
SKUs extracted from seller_custom_field + SELLER_SKU attributes + variation-level (same logic as compat preview)
Register router in app/main.py
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow the exact same pattern as compat.py preview_item endpoint. Import get_item from ml_api. The _resolve_item_seller helper can be copied/adapted from compat.py.

userStories.title: Backend — photo upload endpoint
userStories.description: As a user, I want to upload images to the ML API so they get a permanent picture_id for use across items.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/photos/upload endpoint in photos.py
Accepts multipart/form-data with field 'file' (UploadFile)
Requires a 'seller' query param to determine which access_token to use
Validates file type is JPG/JPEG/PNG (reject others with 400)
Validates file size <= 10MB (reject with 400)
Forwards file to ML API: POST https://api.mercadolibre.com/pictures/items/upload with Authorization Bearer token
Returns the ML response: {id: picture_id, variations: [{size, url, secure_url}]}
Uses require_active_org dependency
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use httpx to forward the multipart upload to ML. Get the seller's access token via _get_token from ml_api.py. The ML endpoint is POST /pictures/items/upload with Content-Type: multipart/form-data.

userStories.title: Backend — search SKU endpoint for photos
userStories.description: As a user, I want to search items by SKU so I can find target items to apply photos to.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/photos/search-sku endpoint in photos.py
Request body: {skus: string[]} with max 50 SKUs validation
Reuses search_sku_all_sellers from app/services/compat_copier.py
Filters sellers by can_copy_to permission (admins get all)
Returns list of {seller_slug, seller_name, item_id, sku, title, thumbnail}
Uses require_active_org dependency
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is almost identical to compat.py search_sku endpoint. Import and reuse search_sku_all_sellers from compat_copier.

userStories.title: Backend — apply photos service logic
userStories.description: As a user, I want to apply a set of photos to multiple items so they all have the same images.
acceptanceCriteria: Create app/services/photo_applier.py with async function apply_photos_to_targets()
Parameters: source_item_id, sku, pictures list [{id?, source?}], targets list [{seller_slug, item_id}], user_id, org_id
Creates photo_logs record with status 'processing' before starting
For each target: calls ML API PUT /items/{item_id} with {pictures: [{id: ...} or {source: ...}]} using target seller's access token
Updates photo_logs target entry with 'ok' or error message after each item
Updates photo_logs success_count, error_count, and status ('completed'/'error') when done
Retry on 429 with exponential backoff (3s base, max 5 retries)
Logs errors to api_debug_logs on failure (reuse _log_api_debug pattern from item_copier)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use ml_api._get_token to get access token for each target seller. PUT /items/{item_id} with pictures payload. Follow compat_copier pattern for log updates. Use httpx for the PUT call. The pictures array order defines display order (first = main photo).

userStories.title: Backend — apply photos endpoint + logs endpoint
userStories.description: As a user, I want API endpoints to trigger photo application and view history.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/photos/apply endpoint in photos.py — receives {source_item_id, sku, pictures: [{id?, source?}], targets: [{seller_slug, item_id}]}
Executes apply_photos_to_targets as BackgroundTask
Returns {log_id, status: 'processing', message: 'Fotos sendo aplicadas...'} immediately
GET /api/photos/logs endpoint with query params: limit (default 20), offset (default 0), status (optional filter)
Returns photo_logs rows filtered by org_id, ordered by created_at DESC
Both endpoints use require_active_org dependency
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Same pattern as compat.py copy and logs endpoints. The apply endpoint validates permissions (can_copy_to for each target seller). Use BackgroundTasks from FastAPI.

userStories.title: Frontend — PhotosPage scaffold with preview section
userStories.description: As a user, I want a Photos tab where I can enter an MLB and see the item's photos.
acceptanceCriteria: Create frontend/src/pages/PhotosPage.tsx
Add 'Fotos' tab to App.tsx navigation (visible to all authenticated users), add 'photos' to View type
PhotosPage receives {sellers, headers} props (same as CompatPage)
Input field for MLB ID with parseItemId helper (same format as CompatPage)
Button 'Buscar' triggers GET /api/photos/preview/{item_id}
On success: displays item title, seller, detected SKUs in a Card component
Displays photos in a CSS grid (thumbnails using secure_url, ~150x150px)
Loading spinner while fetching, error message on failure
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow CompatPage.tsx structure closely. Import API_BASE and Seller type from lib/api.ts. Add 'photos' to the View union type in App.tsx and add tab button. Use the Card component from CopyPage.

userStories.title: Frontend — photo delete, restore, and reorder
userStories.description: As a user, I want to delete, restore, and reorder photos in the grid before applying.
acceptanceCriteria: Each photo in the grid has an X button overlay to remove it
Removed photos appear in a 'Removidas' section below with a 'Restaurar' button each
Cannot remove the last remaining photo (X button disabled/hidden when only 1 left)
Photos are reorderable via drag & drop (HTML5 drag events: onDragStart, onDragOver, onDrop)
First photo has a 'Principal' badge overlay
Dragging a photo swaps positions visually in real time
All edits are local state only (no API calls until apply step)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use HTML5 Drag and Drop API — no external library needed. Track active photos as array (ordered), removed photos as separate array. onDragStart: store source index in dataTransfer. onDragOver: preventDefault. onDrop: reorder array by moving source to drop position.

userStories.title: Frontend — add photos by upload and URL
userStories.description: As a user, I want to add new photos by uploading files from my computer or pasting image URLs.
acceptanceCriteria: Button 'Adicionar foto' opens file picker (accept='.jpg,.jpeg,.png')
Supports selecting multiple files at once via file picker
Selected files show local preview in the grid via URL.createObjectURL
Files over 10MB show toast error and are not added
Input field for image URL with 'Adicionar' button
URL images show preview in the grid using the URL as img src
New photos have visual distinction from existing ones (e.g. dashed border or 'Nova' badge)
New photos can be reordered and deleted like existing photos
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Store File objects and URL strings alongside existing ML photo objects in the same photos array. Each photo entry needs a type discriminator: {type: 'existing', id, url} | {type: 'upload', file, previewUrl} | {type: 'url', source}. The actual ML upload happens during the apply step (US-011), not here.

userStories.title: Frontend — SKU search and target selection
userStories.description: As a user, I want to search by SKU and select which items will receive the edited photos.
acceptanceCriteria: SKU input field auto-populates with the first SKU detected from preview
Button 'Buscar por SKU' triggers POST /api/photos/search-sku with {skus: [sku]}
Results displayed as list with checkbox, item_id, title, seller name, current thumbnail
All results selected by default (checkbox checked)
Source item (from preview) is excluded from results automatically (filter by item_id)
Counter shows 'X de Y anúncios selecionados'
Loading state while searching, empty state message if no results
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reuse the search_sku_all_sellers backend which returns {seller_slug, seller_name, item_id, sku, title, thumbnail}. Filter out sourceItemId on the frontend side.

userStories.title: Frontend — apply photos flow with upload and progress
userStories.description: As a user, I want to apply the edited photos to selected targets and see the result.
acceptanceCriteria: 'Aplicar fotos' button enabled only when there are selected targets AND at least 1 photo in the grid
On click: first uploads any 'upload' type photos to POST /api/photos/upload (multipart) to get picture_id
Then calls POST /api/photos/apply with final pictures array: existing as {id}, uploaded as {id: uploaded_picture_id}, URLs as {source}
Button shows 'Aplicando...' and is disabled during operation
On completion: shows summary toast 'X sucesso, Y erros'
Refreshes the logs section after apply completes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Upload flow: for each File in photos array, FormData with 'file' field → POST /api/photos/upload?seller={seller} → get picture_id from response. Build final pictures array mixing {id} and {source} entries. The seller for upload should be the source seller from the preview step.

userStories.title: Frontend — photo operation history section
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see the history of photo operations with status and details.
acceptanceCriteria: History section below the main form, collapsible, open by default
Fetches GET /api/photos/logs on page mount
Each log row shows: date (formatted), source_item_id, SKU, total/success/error counts, status badge (colored)
Clicking a row expands to show per-target results (item_id, seller, ok/error message)
Status filter dropdown: Todos, Sucesso, Erro, Em andamento
Polls GET /api/photos/logs every 5s while any log has status 'processing'
'Carregar mais' button for pagination (offset += 20)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow the exact same pattern as CompatPage history section. Use setInterval(5000) for polling, clearInterval when no processing items. Status badges: 'completed' = green, 'processing' = yellow/blue, 'error' = red.
